overall_assessment.fls_score_context: This 296 FLS score represents solid intermediate performance, placing you in the competent range with room for refinement in precision and speed.
overall_assessment.session_headline: Strong educational demonstration of surgeon's knot technique with excellent hand switching - focus next on speed optimization while maintaining this quality.

rubric_insights.observation: 300-second completion time is methodical but leaves significant room for speed improvement without sacrificing technique quality
rubric_insights.impact_on_score: Approximately 100+ points lost to time - each 30 seconds saved would add ~50 points to final score

rubric_insights.observation: 2mm deviation from each mark resulted in 4-point penalty - good accuracy but perfectible with more deliberate targeting
rubric_insights.impact_on_score: 4 points lost to placement deviation - minimal impact but represents achievable precision gains

rubric_insights.observation: Excellent surgeon's knot formation with proper C-loop technique and consistent hand switching across all throws
rubric_insights.impact_on_score: No points lost to knot quality - this is a significant strength to preserve

technique_coaching.observation: Frames 2-4 show textbook surgeon's knot formation with proper C-loop and reverse C-loop technique, but the 90-second duration for first throw suggests over-deliberation
technique_coaching.why_it_matters: While technique is excellent, excessive time on first throw limits overall efficiency and can cause fatigue in longer procedures
technique_coaching.correction: Practice the C-loop formation until it becomes automatic - aim for 45-60 seconds maximum on first throw while maintaining current quality
technique_coaching.drill: Perform 20 isolated surgeon's knots on a practice board, timing each first throw - target 45 seconds with zero technique compromise

technique_coaching.observation: Frame 6 shows good instrument handling but the 60-second duration for subsequent throws indicates potential inefficiencies in loop formation or tensioning
technique_coaching.why_it_matters: Streamlined subsequent throws are critical for overall time management and maintaining rhythm
technique_coaching.correction: Focus on continuous motion during loop formation - minimize pauses between wrapping and tensioning phases
technique_coaching.drill: Practice 15 rapid single throws after surgeon's knot, aiming for 20-25 seconds each with smooth transitions

technique_coaching.observation: Frame 1 shows good visualization but 2mm deviation from marks suggests need for more precise initial targeting
technique_coaching.why_it_matters: Precise placement reduces tissue trauma and ensures optimal closure strength
technique_coaching.correction: Take an extra 2-3 seconds to align needle perpendicular to tissue before driving through marks
technique_coaching.drill: Practice 25 needle drives through marked targets, measuring deviation - aim for <1mm consistently

strengths_to_reinforce.observation: Frames 2-3 demonstrate exceptional understanding of surgeon's knot mechanics with clear C-loop and reverse C-loop formation
strengths_to_reinforce.why_it_matters: This foundational technique prevents knot slippage and is essential for surgical safety
strengths_to_reinforce.encouragement: Your surgeon's knot technique is textbook perfect - this level of understanding will serve you well as you focus on speed optimization

strengths_to_reinforce.observation: Frames 5-6 show excellent hand switching consistency, maintaining proper technique throughout all throws
strengths_to_reinforce.why_it_matters: Consistent hand switching prevents knot jamming and ensures even tension distribution
strengths_to_reinforce.encouragement: Your hand switching is smooth and deliberate - this advanced skill puts you ahead of many trainees at your level

strengths_to_reinforce.observation: Frames 9-10 demonstrate clean, controlled cutting technique with proper scissors positioning
strengths_to_reinforce.why_it_matters: Precise cutting prevents suture damage and maintains knot integrity
strengths_to_reinforce.encouragement: Your cutting technique is confident and controlled - excellent attention to this often-overlooked detail

practice_plan.session_focus: Speed optimization while preserving your excellent technique quality
practice_plan.warmup_drill: 5 minutes of rapid needle loading at 2/3 point - build muscle memory for consistent grasp

primary_drill.name: Timed Surgeon's Knot Progression
primary_drill.description: 1) Set timer for 45 seconds, complete surgeon's knot only. 2) Add one single throw, target 65 seconds total. 3) Add second single throw, target 85 seconds total. 4) Complete full sequence with cutting, target 120 seconds total
primary_drill.success_criteria: Hit time targets on 6/8 attempts while maintaining current knot quality and <2mm placement accuracy

practice_plan.cooldown: Review your fastest successful attempt - identify which movements felt most efficient and which felt rushed
practice_plan.estimated_sessions_to_improvement: 4-6 focused sessions should achieve sub-180 second times with current quality

progress_context.vs_baseline: Significant improvement from early 28-point performance - technique mastery is clearly developing
progress_context.vs_peak: This 296 score represents solid mid-range performance in your progression, with clear upward trajectory potential
progress_context.milestone_note: Next target: Sub-180 seconds with zero penalties would put you in the 400+ FLS range

coaching_limitations: This appears to be an educational demonstration video with overlays, which may not reflect actual trainee performance timing or natural working rhythm - recommendations assume this represents actual technique execution speed
